context.role: autonomous grooming behavior, occurs randomly (30% chance) from idle state
context.usage_scenario: plays for 5 seconds as natural bird behavior, shows Echo is content and self-caring

visual_specification.shape: cockatiel with head turned to side/back, grooming feathers
visual_specification.texture: pixel art with minimal dithering, hand-drawn organic feel
visual_specification.outline: 1px dark outline (#1D2B53) on key features only

animation.description: F1: head turns to side, F2-3: beak touches wing/back feathers, F4: head shifts to other side, F5: return to center

visual_specification.details: head rotated to reach back/wing feathers, beak touches body, subtle feather particles (#FFA300) drift sideways, crest in normal position, eyes half-closed in concentration

relationships.shared_with: all Echo states share same base body structure and color palette
relationships.differences_from: unique head-turned pose, beak-to-body contact, accompanied by feather particle effects, autonomous behavior (not player-triggered)

explicit_negations: no motion blur or tweening between frames
no excessive feather particles (2-3 max at a time)
no dramatic contortions (keep natural bird grooming pose)
no visible dirt or parasites (just grooming behavior)
